ranked_movies.title: Se7en
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a top-tier psychological thriller and a quintessential 90s film. Consistently recommended across multiple threads for its dark tone and masterful execution.

ranked_movies.title: The Silence of the Lambs
ranked_movies.reason: Widely cited as a benchmark for the crime thriller genre. Praised for its psychological depth and is a common point of reference in discussions about serial killer films.

ranked_movies.title: No Country for Old Men
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for its unique villain and intense thriller elements. Mentioned in the context of both its coin-flipping antagonist and its overall quality.

ranked_movies.title: Prisoners
ranked_movies.reason: Described as an 'edge-of-your-seat thriller' and specifically recommended for fans of intense, gripping crime dramas.

ranked_movies.title: Zodiac
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its detailed, psychological take on a true crime story and its strong cinematography, fitting the request for well-shot thrillers.

ranked_movies.title: The Usual Suspects
ranked_movies.reason: A classic twist-filled crime thriller that is repeatedly suggested as a must-watch, often with the advice to go in knowing nothing about it.

ranked_movies.title: Big Trouble in Little China
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a major discussion thread. While opinions are mixed, it is celebrated by many as a fun, unique, and highly rewatchable cult classic action-comedy.

ranked_movies.title: Memento
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended for its unique, disorienting narrative structure and as a prime example of a psychological thriller.

ranked_movies.title: Clear and Present Danger
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as a solid 90s political action-thriller, with specific acclaim for its intense convoy ambush scene and being one of the last great Jack Ryan films.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in a list of top-tier crime thrillers, indicating its high regard within the genre for its brutal and intense narrative.

ranked_movies.title: Nightcrawler
ranked_movies.reason: Included in a list of highly recommended crime thrillers, noted for its dark commentary and Jake Gyllenhaal's performance.

ranked_movies.title: Gone Girl
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned alongside other major psychological thrillers, recognized for its twisty plot and themes of manipulation.

ranked_movies.title: The Game
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a mind-bending psychological thriller from the 90s, fitting the request for suspenseful and unpredictable films.

ranked_movies.title: Heat
ranked_movies.reason: Included in a list of essential crime thrillers, renowned for its iconic shootout scenes and the dynamic between Pacino and De Niro.

ranked_movies.title: Sicario
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a modern, intense crime thriller with strong direction and suspenseful set pieces.

ranked_movies.title: Jack Reacher
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a dedicated thread. While debated for its fidelity to the source material, it is widely agreed to be a very well-made and entertaining crime thriller on its own merits.

ranked_movies.title: Mulholland Drive
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for fans of David Lynch's style, cited as a masterpiece of surreal psychological horror and mystery.

ranked_movies.title: Prospect
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a hidden gem sci-fi thriller that 'flew under the radar,' praised for its world-building and strong performances on a low budget.

ranked_movies.title: The Others
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a great horror/thriller with minimal body horror, known for its atmospheric tension and classic twist ending.

ranked_movies.title: Orphan
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for a request for 'melodramatic horror/thriller' movies, noted for its scandalous plot and entertaining, over-the-top drama.
